persona: Busy parent running a birthday party booth
issue: Party Mode is the parent-ready booth flow, but it is Pro/native-gated and still not fully hands-off.
evidence: Agent review: Party Mode is locked behind Pro/native and auto-reset pauses after Save / Share.
recommendation: Make a basic free Guest Mode with simple reset and hidden settings, then charge only for advanced event controls.

persona: Busy parent running a birthday party booth
issue: The host can start a guest flow while autosave is off, risking lost party memories.
evidence: Agent review: Party setup can start while autosave is off; Photos save is opt-in.
recommendation: Before any guest mode starts, require a save destination choice and show a saved-count status.

persona: Busy parent running a birthday party booth
issue: There is no easy guest pickup path, so the parent becomes the delivery bottleneck.
evidence: Agent review: no QR, link, email, SMS, or guest pickup; each output is shared from the host phone.
recommendation: Add a consumer-grade handoff: AirDrop-first guidance, QR/local pickup, or a party export bundle.

persona: Bride or wedding coordinator setting up a casual reception booth
issue: Print is not a real print workflow; it exports an image and leaves physical printing to the user.
evidence: Agent review: Print tab produces a PNG, with no AirPrint, paper size, bleed, or printer setup.
recommendation: For consumer launch, rename it to 4x6 sheet export unless AirPrint or clear print handoff is implemented.

persona: Bride or wedding coordinator setting up a casual reception booth
issue: A monthly plan feels wrong for a one-night wedding-style use case.
evidence: Agent review: wedding user expected event/day pass or one-time unlock, not monthly billing.
recommendation: Use one-time unlock or event pass until there is recurring consumer content value.

persona: Venue or bar owner evaluating photo-booth promo value
issue: Business branding needs are far beyond the current consumer app.
evidence: Agent review: no logo upload, brand kit, campaign container, or venue CTA.
recommendation: Keep this out of the launch backlog; revisit only if BoothBop pursues venues later.

issue: The home screen presents too many jobs before the user has taken one photo.
evidence: Agent review: Take Photos, Browse Templates, Party Setup, My Photos, Import, and install/dev controls make the app feel like a control panel.
recommendation: Make first launch mostly Start Booth plus small Templates/My Photos access; move Party Setup and Import out of the primary stack.

issue: GIF, Boom, Video, Print, retake, and styling are hidden behind a generic Edit button.
evidence: Agent review: default review screen only exposes the preview, Edit, Save / Share, and Take Again.
recommendation: Show output tabs on the main review surface and reserve Edit for styling controls.

issue: Save / Share is too vague for the most important post-capture action.
evidence: Agent review: users cannot tell whether it saves to Photos, downloads, or opens a share sheet.
recommendation: Split Save to Photos/Download and Share, with destination-specific success copy.

issue: The app lacks a free, first-class 9:16 Story/Reels/TikTok output.
evidence: Agent review: Story layout is buried in layout controls and treated as Pro.
recommendation: Make one basic 9:16 story canvas free; monetize premium social packs instead.

issue: Boomerang is created from still frames and will feel like a slideshow, not a social-native motion capture.
evidence: Agent review: current GIF/Boom outputs use the four captured stills forward/reverse.
recommendation: Add a true burst/video capture mode before targeting social creators.

persona: Corporate event marketer needing branded content
issue: Corporate lead capture, brand controls, and ROI reporting are absent.
evidence: Agent review: no lead forms, consent, logo upload, CRM export, branded gallery, or analytics.
recommendation: Do not let this drive launch; this is a separate B2B product direction.

issue: Professional print, camera, lighting, and event reliability expectations are outside the consumer launch scope.
evidence: Agent review: no printer queue, DNP/Selphy guidance, DSLR/external camera support, DPI/bleed, or redundant storage.
recommendation: Keep professional operator needs out of the consumer launch roadmap unless the strategy changes.

persona: Casual App Store user comparing photo booth apps
issue: The App Store promise includes strip, GIF, boomerang, print, and video, but the post-shoot screen hides those outputs behind Edit.
evidence: Agent review: visible controls after capture are Edit, Save / Share, and Take Again; output tabs live inside the edit drawer.
recommendation: Put output selection directly under the preview and make the primary save button reflect the selected format.

persona: Casual App Store user comparing photo booth apps
issue: Free/privacy copy does not clearly disclose the Pro tier, watermarks, and gated features.
evidence: Agent review: listing leans on no-catch language while Pro gates templates, layouts, captions, quality, print, Party Mode, and watermark removal.
recommendation: Rewrite App Store and in-app copy with a precise free/Pro split before launch.

persona: Casual App Store user comparing photo booth apps
issue: BoothBop has presets, not the lightweight designer consumers may expect from competing photo booth apps.
evidence: Agent review: no custom text placement, logo/background upload, color picker, or saved user template designer.
recommendation: Either position BoothBop as intentionally simple or add basic crop/text/sticker controls.

issue: The app claims BoothBop never stores photos while completed sessions are saved locally in the app gallery.
evidence: Agent review cited ReviewScreen copy and saveSession/IndexedDB storage path.
recommendation: Change copy to: never uploaded, saved locally in BoothBop Gallery on this device until deleted.

issue: Sessions and imported photos are saved locally without an explicit first-run persistence choice.
evidence: Agent review: capture and import paths save sessions automatically; no first-run local-storage disclosure.
recommendation: Add first-run privacy sheet and a Temporary Session mode.

issue: Clear all can be misunderstood as deleting all device copies, but it only clears BoothBop's local gallery.
evidence: Agent review: clearSessions clears IndexedDB only; Photos/download/shared/cache files remain.
recommendation: Rename to Clear BoothBop Gallery and explicitly state Photos, downloads, and shared copies remain.

issue: Reopened saved sessions are downscaled, undermining later reprints.
evidence: Agent review: blobToCanvas defaults to 720px when reopening saved blobs, below standard/high capture quality.
recommendation: Preserve natural blob dimensions and store dimensions with each session.

issue: The print sheet is aspect-correct but not truly print-ready.
evidence: Agent review: PNG export has no DPI metadata, PDF, bleed, trim marks, safe area, or printer preset.
recommendation: Rename as 4x6 PNG sheet or add real print-size guidance and print metadata/handoff.

issue: Users cannot fix composition because every image is center-cropped to a square.
evidence: Agent review: captureSquareFrame and importPhotos center-crop; review only supports retake/reorder.
recommendation: Add per-shot crop/reposition controls before targeting keepsake users.

persona: Accessibility-sensitive low-vision user with mild motor impairment
issue: The UI relies on condensed uppercase and tiny labels that are hostile for low-vision users.
evidence: Agent review: core fonts and components force uppercase/tracking; many operational labels are text-xs or smaller.
recommendation: Add accessibility/simple mode with larger sentence-case labels and stronger contrast.

persona: Accessibility-sensitive low-vision user with mild motor impairment
issue: There is no obvious Cancel/Stop button during preview or capture.
evidence: Agent review: escape is the BoothBop wordmark; capture screen lacks visible cancel/stop control.
recommendation: Add a large Cancel/Stop control during preview and capture.

persona: Accessibility-sensitive low-vision user with mild motor impairment
issue: Capture feedback is mostly visual and motion/flash heavy.
evidence: Agent review: countdown lacks aria-live; flash and pulse animations lack reduced-motion/reduced-flash handling.
recommendation: Add aria-live countdown/status and reduced-motion/no-flash settings.

issue: Web and Android users can hit Pro locks but cannot buy or restore Pro.
evidence: Agent review: ProScreen disables purchase outside native and docs say web/PWA does not sell Pro.
recommendation: Remove Pro locks on web/PWA or provide a clear App Store/native handoff before locked controls are shown.

issue: Copy creates three competing concepts: free full PWA, iOS native app, and iOS-only Pro/Photos features.
evidence: Agent review: InstallCard says full app/no app store/free; Pro and Photos autosave point to iOS app.
recommendation: Use platform-specific copy and hide iOS-native features from Android/web flows.

issue: Web save behavior does not match phone photo-app expectations.
evidence: Agent review: web falls back to downloads/share sheet; Photos autosave is native-only.
recommendation: Label web actions as Download and Share; show destination after save.

issue: The current consumer Pro subscription is not launchable as monthly.
evidence: Agent review: Pro sells static templates, Party Mode, HD, captions, and watermark-free exports; docs say monthly needs recurring creative value.
recommendation: Ship one-time unlock or delay monthly until recurring consumer value exists.

issue: Take Photos is a two-step path: home opens preview, then the user taps again to start countdown.
evidence: Agent review: openCamera sets preview; CameraScreen renders another Take Photos button.
recommendation: Auto-start after camera is ready or relabel the second action Start Countdown with a clear reason.

issue: The first screen does not show the desirable finished artifact before asking for permission/action.
evidence: Agent review: production home shows logo, pitch, and action stack, not sample strip/GIF result.
recommendation: Show a polished sample strip or GIF preview in the first viewport.

persona: Budget-conscious college student and subscription skeptic
issue: Monthly pricing reads like a one-time watermark/template unlock stretched into rent.
evidence: Agent review: current Pro scope is static templates, captions, HD, Party Mode, and watermark removal.
recommendation: Use one-time unlock or paid packs until there is recurring value students repeatedly use.

persona: Budget-conscious college student and subscription skeptic
issue: Home promises 30 looks, but many are Pro, creating a bait-and-switch impression.
evidence: Agent review: gallery marks Free/Pro only after entering; many catalog entries are pro.
recommendation: Change copy to disclose the free/Pro count or default gallery to free templates first.

persona: Budget-conscious college student and subscription skeptic
issue: The paywall has no proof that Pro improves the user's actual output.
evidence: Agent review: Pro screen is generic bullets, not current-capture before/after.
recommendation: Show the user's current strip/GIF with the locked template, caption, HD, or watermark-free result.

issue: The product is positioned as classic photobooth, not a social creator tool.
evidence: Agent review: home and metadata lead with classic photo booth, parties, weddings, and strips.
recommendation: Do not target social creators until the app has Story-first capture, real motion, and creator templates.

issue: Captioning is a Pro footer field, not a social text tool.
evidence: Agent review: caption is footer-only; no overlay text, font choices, placement, outlines, or safe zones.
recommendation: Add a basic text overlay editor before asking creators to pay.

issue: Template taxonomy is event-booth oriented and not culturally sharp for creators.
evidence: Agent review: categories include birthdays, wedding, graduation, holiday, corporate; no GRWM, photo dump, recap, meme, or bestie packs.
recommendation: Prioritize friend-group and consumer occasion packs for launch; add creator trend packs later.

issue: Graduation guests want keepsakes, but everything is stored and shared from one host device.
evidence: Agent review: no account/QR/cloud/event room; shareCurrent shares only the selected media from the host phone.
recommendation: Add AirDrop-first guest handoff or a post-event export bundle before promising party use.

issue: Graduation support is thin and the free option is too fixed.
evidence: Agent review: four graduation presets exist, but only Class Of '26 is free and custom captions/event names are gated.
recommendation: Ship a graduation starter flow with editable class year, school colors, and at least two strong free templates.

issue: The feature that makes the app safer to hand to guests is Pro-only.
evidence: Agent review: Party Mode requires isPro to hide top-bar actions and enable guest flow.
recommendation: Make basic Guest Mode free; charge for advanced presets, event captions, and automation.

issue: Monthly Pro is mismatched to occasional couple/date-night memory use.
evidence: Agent review: Pro benefits do not create recurring value for a couple making occasional strips.
recommendation: Use one-time unlock or add recurring couple/seasonal packs and shared memory value.

issue: The most emotionally important personalization, a names/date caption, is paywalled.
evidence: Agent review: non-Pro sees Custom footer text is Pro.
recommendation: Allow one free custom caption or names/date field; monetize advanced packs and watermark removal.

issue: Couples may think memories are safely saved when they are only in local app/browser storage.
evidence: Agent review: in-app gallery uses local device storage; Photos autosave is opt-in and native-only.
recommendation: Prompt after capture to Save to Photos and label in-app storage as BoothBop Gallery/local cache.

issue: The app does not provide an obvious guaranteed Save to Photos outcome.
evidence: Agent review: primary action says Save / Share; autosave defaults are off; share sheet or download may not save to camera roll.
recommendation: On iOS native, make Save to Photos primary with a clear success confirmation; put Share secondary.

issue: Pro language sounds like event-host or business software, not family memory software.
evidence: Agent review: Pro sells Party Mode, event names, brand-ready templates; corporate templates are present.
recommendation: Remove corporate/brand/host language from consumer launch surfaces.

issue: My Photos is easy to mistake for the phone Photos app.
evidence: Agent review: gallery is titled My Photos but stores local BoothBop sessions.
recommendation: Rename to BoothBop Gallery and explain when items are or are not saved to Photos.

persona: House-party host who wants unattended guest use
issue: Party Mode breaks the unattended promise because Save / Share pauses auto-reset.
evidence: Agent review: partyActionTaken shows Auto-reset paused after save/share instead of moving to next guest.
recommendation: In Guest Mode, save/share should be non-blocking and return to the next session after a grace period.

persona: House-party host who wants unattended guest use
issue: Party Mode hides GIF/video choice even though those are key app outputs.
evidence: Agent review: output tabs are inside Edit, but Edit is hidden in partyMode; review starts as strip.
recommendation: Let host preselect outputs or show a guest-safe output picker.

persona: House-party host who wants unattended guest use
issue: All-night photo retention feels unsafe because Photos autosave is native-only and opt-in.
evidence: Agent review: local gallery save is best-effort; autosave defaults off; native Photos path unavailable on web.
recommendation: Party setup must require a save destination and show a persistent saved-count status.

persona: Harsh iOS App Store reviewer and refund-risk buyer
issue: Launch materials say no paywall/full app available while the app contains a monthly Pro paywall.
evidence: Agent review cited docs/APP_STORE no-paywall language versus ProScreen and locked Print route.
recommendation: Rewrite App Store/review notes and in-app copy around a transparent free tier plus Pro, or remove Pro from launch.

persona: Harsh iOS App Store reviewer and refund-risk buyer
issue: Print is presented beside free outputs, but tapping it opens Pro for free users.
evidence: Agent review: Review tabs include Print unconditionally; selecting print when not Pro opens Pro.
recommendation: Hide Print until Pro, label it Print Pro, or move it out of the primary output switcher.

persona: Harsh iOS App Store reviewer and refund-risk buyer
issue: The paywall is thin for monthly billing and lacks clear renewal terms/trial/lapse explanation.
evidence: Agent review: small cancel-anytime footer, no clear monthly auto-renewal terms near CTA, no trial state.
recommendation: Add concise Apple billing, renewal, cancellation, no-trial, and feature-lapse terms near the CTA.

persona: Harsh iOS App Store reviewer and refund-risk buyer
issue: Watermark removal is muddled across docs, paywall copy, and implementation.
evidence: Agent review: docs say strip footer stays, paywall says watermark-free animated exports, code appears to pass watermark false to strip for Pro.
recommendation: Define per-format branding rules in UI/docs and show before/after examples.
